title: rosa alpina subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Rosa alpina subsp. pyrenaica, commonly known as Pyrenean rose or Pyrenean alpine rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Rosa alpina subsp. pyrenaica are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. However, I do not have information on the specific therapeutic actions or health benefits associated with this plant.

The plant's extracts have also been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in wound healing, preventing infections, and even treating skin conditions like acne and eczema. Furthermore, Rosa alpina subsp. pyrenaica has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues, such as nausea, bloating, and diarrhea, and its anti-inflammatory properties have been shown to provide relief from menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. These constituents, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, improving the lives of individuals by reducing the risk of chronic diseases and promoting wound healing. The leaves are rich in salicylates, compounds similar to aspirin, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. side_effects: Rosa alpina subsp. pyrenaica can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues.
